3) Effect of Diffusion Step: To further explore the impact
of reverse diffusion steps on model training performance,
this study conducted ablation experiments, comparing the
experimental results of the proposed method, UTD-PTP (using
two steps of diffusion based on DDIM), with the PD-PTP-100
method (using 100 steps of diffusion based on DDPM),
as shown in Table II. In addition to PD-PTP-100, experiments
were conducted with reverse diffusion step sizes of 90 and
110. Taking the BIT subdataset as an example, despite the
decrease in ADE from 5.18 to 4.71 and FDE from 4.65 to
3.78 as the reverse diffusion step size increased from 90 to 110,
the predictive performance gradually improved, accompanied
by an increase in model inference time. Based on the results
for A VERAGE-T and A VERAGE-A, it can be observed that
the model performance of UTD-PTP is close to PD-PTP-100,
with even
